docs.title: Kamal's Server
docs.text:
This is the stuff I run on my personal server at home. It's a little Ubuntu box that never sleeps — hosts my cloud storage, a private search engine, some game servers, and all the automation glue that makes life easier.

This site is mostly for friends and family to see what I've been building. Technical documentation lives elsewhere.

docs.title: At a glance
docs.text:
What I use the server for:

  • ☁️ Cloud storage — my own Nextcloud, no big tech involved
  • 🔍 Private search — SearXNG, no tracking
  • 📰 RSS reader — FreshRSS for following blogs and news
  • 🎮 Game servers — retro arcade + multiplayer FPS
  • 🤖 AI agent — Hermes Agent automates stuff for me
  • 📸 Photo gallery — PiGallery2 for my photos
  • 🔔 Push notifications — ntfy pings my phone
  • 💾 File sync — Syncthing keeps devices in sync
  • 🗂️ File management — web-based file browser
  • 📋 Dashboard — Homepage showing everything at a glance

docs.title: On this site
docs.text:
• Apps — what apps I run and what they do
  • Projects — things I've built
  • Tech Stack — what technologies make it all work

docs.title: Apps
docs.text: All the self-hosted apps running on my server. Every one is open source and runs in Docker.

docs.title: Nextcloud — Cloud Storage
docs.text:
My personal Dropbox. Files, photos, contacts, calendar — all self-hosted. Uses PostgreSQL for the database and Redis for caching. Has a cron job that runs nightly maintenance.

What I use it for: Syncing files between devices, sharing large files with friends, backing up phone photos automatically.

docs.title: SearXNG — Private Search Engine
docs.text:
A meta-search engine that queries multiple backends (Google, DuckDuckGo, Wikipedia, etc.) and returns results without tracking you. No logs, no profiling, no ads.

What I use it for: Searching the web without being the product.

docs.title: FreshRSS — RSS Reader
docs.text:
Fetches and displays RSS feeds from websites and blogs I follow. Has a great mobile app (FeedMe) that connects to it.

What I use it for: Staying up to date with blogs, news sites, and content creators without algorithmic feeds.

docs.title: Homepage — Dashboard
docs.text:
A clean, customizable startpage that shows all my services in one place. Auto-discovers running Docker containers and shows their status.

What I use it for: A browser homepage with quick access to everything.

docs.title: Obsidian — Notes
docs.text:
My note-taking app, running in a container so I can access it from any browser. Syncs across devices via Syncthing.

What I use it for: All my notes, journaling, project planning. Hermes Agent also writes to my vault automatically.

docs.title: Ntfy — Push Notifications
docs.text:
Sends push notifications to my phone. Simple HTTP API — any script or automation can send me a ping.

What I use it for: Hermes cron jobs send me alerts (server health issues, daily briefings). My own scripts use it too.

docs.title: Syncthing — File Sync
docs.text:
Decentralized file synchronization. No cloud, no central server — devices sync directly peer-to-peer.

What I use it for: Keeping my Obsidian vault, photos, and documents in sync across phone, laptop, and server.

docs.title: PiGallery2 — Photo Gallery
docs.text:
A beautiful self-hosted photo gallery. Scans photo directories and generates thumbnails automatically.

What I use it for: Browsing and sharing my photo collection.

docs.title: Portainer — Docker Manager
docs.text:
Web UI for managing Docker containers, images, volumes, and networks.

What I use it for: Quick container troubleshooting without SSH-ing.

docs.title: FileBrowser
docs.text:
A simple web-based file manager. Upload, download, delete, and preview files from any browser.

What I use it for: Quick file management without needing to SSH in.

docs.title: Epic Games Claimer
docs.text:
Automatically claims free games from the Epic Games Store so I never miss one. Runs on a schedule and notifies me when it claims something.

What I use it for: Growing my game library without remembering to check the store.

docs.title: Hermes Workspace
docs.text:
A browser-based development environment with a code editor and terminal. Accessible from any device with a browser.

What I use it for: Quick code edits and scripting on the go.

docs.title: Hermes Agent — AI Assistant
docs.text:
My autonomous AI agent (powered by Nous Research's Hermes). It runs on the server 24/7 and automates tasks for me — monitoring, reporting, research, content generation. It connects to Telegram so I can talk to it from my phone.

What I use it for: Morning briefings, server health monitoring, automated research, note-taking, and way too many other things.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text: Things I've built that run on the server.

docs.title: BlockOps — Multiplayer FPS
docs.text:
A browser-based voxel-style multiplayer first-person shooter. Built with Node.js and Socket.IO for real-time networking. Friends can hop in from a browser — no install needed.

URL: games.kamalrajnaidu.com/blockops/ Tech: Node.js, Socket.IO, Three.js

docs.title: Emulator Arcade — Retro Games
docs.text:
A retro game emulation arcade playable in any browser. Uses EmulatorJS with RetroArch compiled to WebAssembly — the emulation runs entirely on your device (no server-side rendering).

URL: games.kamalrajnaidu.com/ Tech: Node.js, EmulatorJS, RetroArch WASM

docs.title: Blog
docs.text:
My personal blog — thoughts, projects, and whatever I feel like writing about.

URL: blog.kamalrajnaidu.com

docs.title: Hub Page
docs.text:
My main personal website / portfolio.

URL: https://kamalrajnaidu.com

docs.title: Dev Dashboard
docs.text: A developer-focused dashboard with tools and utilities I use daily.

docs.title: Hermes WebUI
docs.text: The browser interface for Hermes Agent — the chat interface I talk to from this site.

docs.title: Particle Playground
docs.text: Creative coding experiments with canvas particles and visual effects.

docs.title: BeatLab Deploy
docs.text: Deployment configuration for BeatLab, a music production tool project.

docs.title: Tech Stack
docs.text: What runs under the hood.

docs.title: Operating System
docs.text:
• OS: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS
  • Kernel: 6.x series

docs.title: Container Runtime
docs.text:
• Docker with containerd
  • Composed stacks managed via CasaOS (self-hosted app platform)

docs.title: Web Server
docs.text: • nginx — reverse proxy and static file server

docs.title: Hermes Agent
docs.text:
• Hermes by Nous Research — autonomous AI agent framework
  • Providers: OpenRouter, Gemini, OpenCode
  • Connected platforms: Telegram, ntfy, REST API
  • 8 cron jobs: health monitoring, daily briefings, research, session management, backup

docs.title: Networking
docs.text:
• Cloudflare — DNS, proxy, DDoS protection, TLS
  • Cloudflare Tunnel — secure ingress for private services
  • nginx — subdomain routing and reverse proxying

docs.title: Databases
docs.text:
• PostgreSQL 14 — Nextcloud database
  • Redis 6 — Nextcloud caching
  • SQLite — various apps (FreshRSS, FileBrowser, PiGallery2)

docs.title: Storage & Sync
docs.text:
• Samba — local network file sharing
  • Syncthing — P2P device sync (phone, laptop, server)

docs.title: Monitoring & Automation
docs.text:
• Hermes Agent cron — 8 automated jobs (watchdog, health reports, daily briefings, session management)
  • System cron — daily backups at 4am
  • Ntfy — push notification delivery to phone

docs.title: Development
docs.text:
• Node.js
  • Python 3
  • Shell scripting (Bash)
  • Git / GitHub

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text: A custom-built PC running 24/7 at home. SSD storage, NVIDIA GPU, wired ethernet connection.
